

We want to integrate f(x) = $e^{x}*e^{-2x}$ (pretend we can't find this analytically). 

Naive monte carlo:
```{r}
N <- 1000
xs <- rexp(N, rate = 2)
ws <- exp(xs)/2

i_hat <- (1/N)*sum(ws)
se_hat <- sqrt(((var(ws)/N)))

cat(paste0("MC Estimate: ", round(i_hat, 2), "\n",
      "Standard Error: ", round(se_hat, 4)))
```

Importance sampling: choosing a function g(x) to downweight the chance we choose from low draws of g

Choose g such that p ~ exponential(1.5) 
```{r}
N <- 1000
xs <- rexp(N, rate = 1.5)
ws <- exp(xs/2)/1.5

i_hat <- (1/N)*sum(ws)
se_hat <- sqrt(((var(ws)/N)))

cat(paste0("MC Estimate: ", round(i_hat, 2), "\n",
      "Standard Error: ", round(se_hat, 4)))
```

Choose g such that p ~ exponential(1.01) 
```{r}
N <- 1000
xs <- rexp(N, rate = 1.01)
ws <- exp(0.01*xs)/1.01

i_hat <- (1/N)*sum(ws)
se_hat <- sqrt(((var(ws)/N)))

cat(paste0("MC Estimate: ", round(i_hat, 2), "\n",
      "Standard Error: ", round(se_hat, 4)))
```
